16	//
17	// Trailing blank line keeps a one-line gap between the header and
18	// user-visible content; without it the body's first heading would
19	// fold into the comment block on some markdown renderers.
20	const managedHeaderTemplate = "<!-- Managed by agent-sync — do not edit. Source: {source-url}@{short-sha}. Regenerate: agent-sync sync -->\n\n"
21	
22	// jsonSidecarBody is the body for the .agent-sync-managed sidecar marker
23	// the claude adapter writes next to .mcp.json. JSON has no comment
24	// syntax, so the sidecar advertises ownership instead of editing
25	// the JSON file with a comment.
26	//
27	// The body is intentionally short and human-readable; it is not
28	// machine-parsed by anything in v1.
29	const jsonSidecarBody = `Managed by agent-sync.
30	
31	The sibling .mcp.json file contains entries owned by agent-sync under
32	the /mcpServers/agentsync_<id> JSON pointers. Other entries are
33	preserved across syncs.
34	
35	To remove agent-sync-managed entries: agent-sync unmanage claude
36	`
37	
38	// markdownHeader returns the managed-file banner used at the top of
39	// every owned markdown file (rules, commands, skills). Content is
40	// constant in v1; future versions may inline real source URL +
41	// short SHA.
42	func markdownHeader() []byte {
43		return []byte(managedHeaderTemplate)
44	}
45	
46	// jsonSidecarMarker returns the body for the .agent-sync-managed sidecar
47	// marker placed next to strict-JSON tool-owned files (currently just
48	// .mcp.json). See managedHeaderTemplate doc for why JSON gets a
49	// sidecar instead of an inline comment.
50	func jsonSidecarMarker() []byte {
51		return []byte(jsonSidecarBody)
52	}
53	
54	// sectionMarkerBegin returns the opening marker for a managed section
55	// inside a tool-owned markdown file (currently just CLAUDE.md). The
56	// marker is HTML-comment shaped so any markdown renderer treats it
57	// as invisible.
58	//
59	// id MUST satisfy the IR id grammar; an invalid id is a programming
60	// error inside the adapter (the IR decoder rejects bad ids upstream)
61	// and panics here so the failure surfaces immediately rather than
62	// silently corrupting CLAUDE.md.
63	func sectionMarkerBegin(id string) []byte {
64		mustValidID(id)
65		return []byte("<!-- agent-sync:begin id=" + id + " -->")
66	}
67	
68	// sectionMarkerEnd returns the closing marker. See sectionMarkerBegin
69	// for the id-validation contract.
70	func sectionMarkerEnd(id string) []byte {
71		mustValidID(id)
72		return []byte("<!-- agent-sync:end id=" + id + " -->")
73	}
74	
75	// wrapManagedSection wraps body bytes between the begin/end markers
76	// for the supplied id and returns the full slice ready for a
77	// write_tool_owned op. A trailing newline after the end marker keeps
78	// the merged file POSIX-clean if it is the last entry.
79	func wrapManagedSection(id string, body []byte) []byte {
80		begin := sectionMarkerBegin(id)
81		end := sectionMarkerEnd(id)
82		out := make([]byte, 0, len(begin)+len(body)+len(end)+3)
83		out = append(out, begin...)
84		out = append(out, '\n')
85		out = append(out, body...)
86		if len(body) == 0 || body[len(body)-1] != '\n' {
87			out = append(out, '\n')
88		}
89		out = append(out, end...)
90		out = append(out, '\n')
91		return out
92	}
93	
94	// readmeForSubdir returns the README.md body the claude adapter
95	// emits into each reserved subdirectory. The README explains
96	// ownership and the exit path so a human stumbling into
97	// .claude/rules/agent-sync/README.md knows it isn't user content.
98	//
99	// subdirLabel is the relative path used inside the body for clarity
100	// (e.g., ".claude/rules/agent-sync"); pass the same string used in the
101	// declared output.
102	func readmeForSubdir(subdirLabel string) []byte {
103		subdirLabel = strings.TrimSpace(subdirLabel)
104		if subdirLabel == "" {
105			// Defensive default. Empty subdirLabel is a programmer error;
106			// an empty README is less harmful than a panic during a sync
107			// that's already trying to recover.
108			subdirLabel = "(unknown subdirectory)"
109		}
110		return fmt.Appendf(nil,
111			"# Managed by agent-sync\n\n"+
112				"Files inside `%s/` are owned by the agent-sync `claude` adapter.\n"+
113				"Editing them by hand is detected as drift on the next sync\n"+
114				"and the changes are overwritten.\n\n"+
115				"To remove every agent-sync-owned file in this directory and\n"+
116				"unbind it from the workspace, run:\n\n"+
117				"    agent-sync unmanage claude\n",
118			subdirLabel,
119		)
120	}
121	
122	// mustValidID panics with a programmer-targeted message when id
123	// does not satisfy the IR id grammar. The IR decoder catches user
124	// inputs upstream; this guard is for adapter bugs.
125	func mustValidID(id string) {
126		if !ir.IsValidID(id) {
127			panic(fmt.Sprintf("claude: invalid id %q reached marker construction (IR decoder should have rejected it upstream)", id))
128		}
129	}
130

17	// claudeReqOn builds a Request syncing the given nodes through the real claude
18	// adapter against an existing workspace path (so a sequence of syncs can run
19	// against the same tree).
20	func claudeReqOn(t *testing.T, ws string, nodes []ir.Node) (Request, func()) {
21		t.Helper()
22		root, err := fsroot.OpenWorkspaceRoot(ws)
23		if err != nil {
24			t.Fatalf("OpenWorkspaceRoot: %v", err)
25		}
26		reg, err := adapter.DiscoverAdapters(context.Background(), adapter.DiscoverOptions{
27			Bundled: []*adapter.BundledAdapter{claudeadapter.Bundled()},
28		})
29		if err != nil {
30			t.Fatalf("DiscoverAdapters: %v", err)
31		}
32		req := Request{
33			Root:          root,
34			WorkspacePath: ws,
35			Registry:      reg,
36			Targets:       []string{"claude"},
37			Nodes:         nodes,
38			Commit:        testCommit,
39			Options:       Options{Now: fixedNow()},
40		}
41		return req, func() { _ = root.Close() }
42	}

44	// TestSync_SharedSubdir_PreservesForeignSkill is the Unit 11 / shared-subdir
45	// data-loss regression guard: .claude/skills is a shared tree, so a sync that
46	// adds, then removes, an agent-sync skill must never touch a foreign skill
47	// living alongside it.
48	func TestSync_SharedSubdir_PreservesForeignSkill(t *testing.T) {
49		ws := t.TempDir()
50	
51		// Seed a foreign skill the user/another tool placed under the shared tree;
52		// it is NOT in any agent-sync ledger.
53		foreign := filepath.Join(ws, ".claude", "skills", "user-thing", "SKILL.md")
54		const foreignBody = "# User's own skill\n\nDo not delete me.\n"
55		if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(foreign), 0o755); err != nil {
56			t.Fatal(err)
57		}
58		if err := os.WriteFile(foreign, []byte(foreignBody), 0o644); err != nil {
59			t.Fatal(err)
60		}
61	
62		// Sync 1: add an agent-sync skill.
63		req1, done1 := claudeReqOn(t, ws, []ir.Node{
64			{ID: "greeter", Kind: ir.KindSkill, Version: 1, Body: []byte("# Greeter")},
65		})
66		if summary, err := Sync(context.Background(), req1); err != nil {
67			t.Fatalf("sync 1: %v", err)
68		} else if summary.Outcome.ExitCode != 0 {
69			t.Fatalf("sync 1 exit = %d, want 0 (%+v)", summary.Outcome.ExitCode, summary.Outcome)
70		}
71		done1()
72	
73		// The agent-sync skill landed under its agent-sync- leaf...
74		agentSyncSkill := filepath.Join(ws, ".claude", "skills", "agent-sync-greeter", "SKILL.md")
75		if _, err := os.Stat(agentSyncSkill); err != nil {
76			t.Fatalf("expected agent-sync skill at %s: %v", agentSyncSkill, err)
77		}
78		// ...and the foreign skill is byte-identical after the swap.
79		assertFileBytes(t, foreign, foreignBody, "after sync 1 (add)")
80	
81		// Sync 2: remove the agent-sync skill (no nodes). Its leaf is orphan-deleted;
82		// the foreign skill must survive untouched.
83		req2, done2 := claudeReqOn(t, ws, nil)
84		if summary, err := Sync(context.Background(), req2); err != nil {
85			t.Fatalf("sync 2: %v", err)
86		} else if summary.Outcome.ExitCode != 0 {
87			t.Fatalf("sync 2 exit = %d, want 0 (%+v)", summary.Outcome.ExitCode, summary.Outcome)
88		}
89		done2()
90	
91		// The agent-sync skill file is gone (orphan-removed)...
92		if _, err := os.Stat(agentSyncSkill); !os.IsNotExist(err) {
93			t.Fatalf("agent-sync skill should be orphan-removed; stat err = %v", err)
94		}
95		// ...and the foreign skill STILL survives.
96		assertFileBytes(t, foreign, foreignBody, "after sync 2 (remove)")
97	}

80	// emitAgentsMD writes the agents-md companion overlay into
81	// CLAUDE.md (workspace root) as a managed section between
82	// <!-- agent-sync:begin id=<id> --> and <!-- agent-sync:end id=<id> -->
83	// markers. CLAUDE.md is tool-owned by Claude Code; user content
84	// outside the managed section is preserved by the merge step
85	// (Unit 12a).
86	//
87	// The body is rejected when it contains the agent-sync marker opener.
88	// Without this guard a body containing `<!-- agent-sync:end id=other -->`
89	// could break out of its own section or forge another section
90	// entirely, leaving the merged CLAUDE.md with conflicting markers
91	// the merge step has no way to resolve safely.
92	//
93	// No managed-file header is added inside the section: the begin/end
94	// markers serve as the equivalent ownership advertisement, and a
95	// header inside a user-owned markdown file would be visually noisy.
96	func emitAgentsMD(emitted *emittedOps, node irNode) error {
97		body, err := decodeBodyOrPassthrough(node.Body)
98		if err != nil {
99			return wrapBodyErr(node, err)
100		}
101		if bytes.Contains(body, markerOpenBytes) {
102			return &adapterkit.Error{
103				Code:    adapterkit.CodeInvalidParams,
104				Message: fmt.Sprintf("claude: agents-md %q body contains agent-sync marker syntax (%q); refusing to corrupt CLAUDE.md", node.ID, string(markerOpenBytes)),
105			}
106		}
107		wrapped := wrapManagedSection(node.ID, body)
108	
109		emitted.add(adapterkit.OpWriteToolOwned{
110			Path:    claudeMDPath,
111			Kind:    adapterkit.ToolOwnedKindMarkdownSection,
112			Locator: sectionIDPrefix + node.ID,
113			Content: wrapped,
114		})
115		return nil
116	}

content.subject: U1: Fix claude agents-md marker double-wrap
content.description: Mirror cursor/codex: emitAgentsMD sends inner body (not wrapManagedSection). Remove now-dead wrapManagedSection/sectionMarker* + header_test cases. Replace stale TestEmitAgentsMD_WrapsBodyInSection to assert inner-body-only. Add engine-level integration test running claude agents-md through real merge. Test-first.

content.subject: U2: Fix tool-owned validate drift (idempotency)
content.description: Extract shared merge dispatch; add non-mutating DrySliceHash in internal/merge. In engine/plan.go OpWriteToolOwned branch, compute desired slice hash and compare to ledger SHA256 (WouldCreate/WouldUpdate/unchanged + out-of-band), mirroring OpWriteFile. Regression tests: sync→validate clean, sync→sync unchanged, out-of-band edit detected, across markdown/json/toml. Test-first.

content.subject: U3: End-to-end dogfood smoke test
content.description: internal/engine/e2e_dogfood_test.go: temp canonical git repo (AGENTS.md + rule + skill), run claude/cursor/codex; assert all sync OK, validate immediately reports no drift, second sync unchanged. Depends on U1+U2.

content.description: docs/quickstart.md (canonical layout, agent-sync frontmatter, init→sync→validate, backing-out note), example canonical repo with correct frontmatter, README adapter-table fix (Codex is bundled) + quickstart link. Contract-level per spec-drift learning.

input.command:
cd /Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/agent-sync
go vet ./internal/adapter/bundled/claude/ ./internal/engine/ 2>&1 | tail -5
git add internal/adapter/bundled/claude/ internal/engine/agents_md_test.go
git commit -q -m "fix(claude): emit inner body for agents-md, not marker-wrapped content

The claude adapter double-wrapped the managed section in CLAUDE.md, but the
engine owns the begin/end markers and rejects pre-wrapped content — so
sync --target claude failed on the most basic AGENTS.md config. codex and
cursor were already fixed; claude was not. Mirror them: send the inner body.

Removes now-dead wrapManagedSection/sectionMarker* helpers and their tests,
replaces the stale unit test that asserted the buggy wrapped output, and adds
an engine-level integration test that runs the claude agents-md emit through
the real merge so this can't regress." && echo "U1 committed"
